title: Livepeer registry manifest
description: Top-level wrapper plus signed manifest content. Published at /.well-known/livepeer-registry.json by the orch-coordinator. Signature is computed by the cold key on secure-orch over the canonical (RFC 8785 JCS) form of the manifest payload.

spec_version.description: Spec-wide SemVer (major.minor or major.minor.patch). Consumers MUST validate the major version; minor and patch are non-breaking by definition. See livepeer-network-protocol/VERSION.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the orch's cold key signed this manifest.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp after which consumers MUST refetch. Resolvers MUST NOT route on expired manifests.

capabilities.description: Flat list of capability tuples. Host is not a registration unit; multi-binary or multi-broker hosts contribute multiple entries here.

eth_address.description: The orch's on-chain identity (Arbitrum One). Signature MUST recover to this address.

service_uri.description: Optional. Diagnostic URL where this manifest is hosted. SHOULD match the on-chain ServiceRegistry entry but is not authoritative; the on-chain entry is the trust anchor.

capability_id.description: Opaque UTF-8 identifier. No closed enum; orchs and gateways may invent new ones (see core belief #1, #7).

offering_id.description: Opaque UTF-8 identifier disambiguating priced tiers under the capability (e.g. different models, hardware classes, SLAs).

interaction_mode.description: Mode name + major version (per Q2 hybrid SemVer), e.g. 'http-stream@v1'. Mode names live in modes/<mode>.md.

price_per_unit_wei.description: Wei per work-unit, expressed as a non-negative decimal string. String form preserves precision beyond JSON's safe-integer range.

worker_url.description: Public TLS endpoint of the broker (or any operator-chosen public-facing proxy that fronts it). MUST be HTTPS. NAT traversal / tunneling is the orch's concern (cloudflared, Traefik, etc.).

extra.description: Orch-declared metadata. Free-form key-value pairs. Gateways use these for workload-specific filtering (region, GPU class, latency tier, etc.). The protocol itself does not interpret extra.

constraints.description: Gateway-side filter hints. Same shape as extra; semantics are gateway-defined.

name.description: The metering dimension's name, e.g. 'tokens', 'video-frame-megapixel', 'barks'. Opaque to the protocol; consumed by gateways for billing display. The extractor (which counts the units) is host-config concern, not advertised here.

signature.title: Cold-key signature
signature.description: Signature is computed over the JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785) form of the manifest payload. Signers: the orch's cold key on secure-orch.

algorithm.description: Signing algorithm. Currently only secp256k1 (Ethereum's curve) is permitted, matching the orch's on-chain identity.

value.description: Hex-encoded signature bytes. For secp256k1: 65 bytes (r || s || v) where v is 27 or 28.

canonicalization.description: JSON canonicalization scheme used before hashing. JCS = RFC 8785.
